channel.title: General Information (new posts)
channel.description: Posts in the forum category "General Information" - Information about PF: the plan, progress that's been made, Frequently Asked Questions, and whatever else may be relevant. Also the place to ask questions about PF.

item.title: PF Account Transfer: Re: PF Account Transfer
item.content:encoded:
We plan to transfer everything that's public over - all your revisions, pages, comments, etc. We'll probably end up reserving all active/contributor account names during and after the transfer. The intent would be for you to be able to reclaim your current username and hopefully not notice any differences. The site will be ours, and we'll have complete control over it, so we'll have no problem doing that.

Once Wikijump (the endpoint of PF) is open, Wikidot will (probably!) still exist - we don't have any control over that. We have most of the international sites backed up and we'll be able to transfer their site to Wikijump if they choose.

Of course you'll still be able to use your Wikidot account to edit Wikidot sites. Any sites that choose to migrate to Wikijump will simply be on a different website.

item.title: PF Account Transfer: PF Account Transfer
item.content:encoded:
So recently I heard about Project Foundation and I have multiple questions regarding the transfer of accounts between the services. Sorry if these questions were already addressed though.

Because it is going to be on a new provider, will we have to make new accounts? I read in the FAQ that almost all articles will be transferred over but what does that mean relating to our accounts. So does this mean it applies to our accounts? Can we just log in the same way we do with Wikidot, or will we have to register new accounts? If we have to register new accounts, what is stopping people from taking account names? The final question is how would are accounts work between the different SCP Foundation Languages? Like would I be able to write on the new service and still go back to the international hub for example and be an active member there?

Sorry if this is all confusing, its just that I recently just joined the site and I am confused. Thanks though.

item.title: Discord, anyone?: Re: Discord, anyone?
item.content:encoded: Noting that as of today, Project Foundation has a Discord server, which is available on request. Please note that it is not a general-purpose server: it is strictly on-topic for PF/Wikijump development (e.g. there is no 'general' channel). If you are interested in contributing or following development, please consider asking for an invitation in #site11.

item.title: Sandbox Shift?: Re: Sandbox Shift?
item.content:encoded: It's a bit far off to really say how it'll go but we'll have the contents of the official sandboxes ported over and I will try and make some of the popular unofficial sandbox owners aware of a mechanism for moving stuff over.

item.title: Sandbox Shift?: Sandbox Shift?
item.content:encoded: I am new to the foundation, and only recently heard of PF. My question is that if we are moving all the scips and tales, what is going to happen with the sandbox sites? Are we just going to leave them there, or are we going to have our own? (personally, i think we should have a saved drafts thing for accounts on the PF site) So basically, what is going to happen to the sandbox?

item.title: Discord, anyone?: Re: Discord, anyone?
item.content:encoded: For the record, we have some unofficial Discord servers that you can join, like SCPDeclassified, DankMemesFromSite19 or more official things, like the server of SCP-INT or the one of the sister site, the Wanderers Library.

item.title: Discord, anyone?: Re: Discord, anyone?
item.content:encoded:
We have an official IRC! It's all cool and shiny and persisting as a protocol for decades!

But no, we don't have an official discord server, and there are no real plans to make one at the moment, as far as I'm aware.

item.title: Discord, anyone?: Discord, anyone?
item.content:encoded: Does the website have an official or unofficial discord server? Just wondering.

item.title: SCP Conversion Project: Re: SCP Conversion Project
item.content:encoded: Interesting project!

item.title: Database overload: Admin Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded: As a general note, instead of replying to your own comments, please edit your previous post using the "edit" function under the "options" tab to the lower right of every comment. That prevents spam buildup, and it's in the rules.

item.title: Database overload: Re: Database overload
item.content:encoded:
Moved to Project Foundation / General Information, as it's not a feature request.

The activities panel is part of our platform Wikidot, which we don't control. Project Foundation is the effort to move away from Wikidot.

I don't think I've ever seen it working. You might be able to find an answer on the Wikidot community forum.

item.title: Database overload: Re: Database overload
item.content:encoded: Just to be clear this is not a major issue as obviously it's still possible to view account activity using a different path. The message has just been up for a while.

item.title: Database overload: Database overload
item.content:encoded: Just curious about the status of the "Due to database overload, the activities panel is temporarily disabled." situation.

item.title: Project Foundation Team?: Re: Project Foundation Team?
item.content:encoded: Just out of curiosity. I sadly don't have anything to offer.

item.title: Project Foundation Team?: Re: Project Foundation Team?
item.content:encoded:
I'm not a member, but may I ask why?

Like, is this just curiosity? Or do you think you have something to offer?

item.title: Project Foundation Team?: Project Foundation Team?
item.content:encoded: I just want to know who are the members of the Project Foundation Team, to see how far is this project has began/self-sustaining the new site can be.

item.title: SCP Conversion Project: Re: SCP Conversion Project
item.content:encoded: that is pretty cool.

item.title: Nerd Stuff: Episode 1: Re: Nerd Stuff: Episode 1
item.content:encoded: Even as someone who's been mucking about in the codebase, I found listening to this really helpful for extra understanding of what's going on.

item.title: Nerd Stuff: Episode 1: Nerd Stuff: Episode 1
item.content:encoded:
Here's what we've been up to since late May.

Nerd Stuff, Episode 1

item.title: Release date?: Release date?
item.content:encoded: Yeah, so I'm curious how far the team's gone. Also, is there a way to see what you've made so far?
